brief.title: HR & People Analytics Performance Dashboard
brief.data_context: Data sourced from internal hr & people analytics systems. Updated weekly. Covers the last 12 months with historical comparison.

recommendation.context_summary: This dashboard serves Financial Controllers in the HR & People Analytics sector. The primary goal is to track recruitment pipeline and time-to-hire. Data is refreshed weekly, and the target users have beginner data literacy. The design should prioritize clarity and actionability over analytical depth.

kpi_task_chart_mapping.rationale: A table provides precise Absenteeism Rate values for users who need exact numbers.
A KPI card gives immediate visibility into the current Internal Promotion Rate value against its target.
Area Chart is appropriate for the task of 'Track against target' with Cost per Hire.
A gauge chart shows Headcount by Department performance relative to a defined threshold or goal.

layout_hierarchy.pattern: Executive summary strip at top. Two-column layout: charts left, KPI cards and commentary right.
layout_hierarchy.primary_section: KPI scorecards for Absenteeism Rate, Internal Promotion Rate
layout_hierarchy.secondary_section: Trend analysis for Absenteeism Rate over time

labels_scales_colors.color_palette: Minimal: White background, single accent color (#E74C3C), grey for secondary data
labels_scales_colors.axis_labels: Always label axes with units (e.g., Revenue in EUR, Time in Days)
labels_scales_colors.number_format: Percentages: 1 decimal place (e.g., 23.4%). Large numbers: K/M suffix (e.g., 1.2M).
labels_scales_colors.scale_type: Linear for most KPIs; logarithmic if data spans multiple orders of magnitude
labels_scales_colors.legend: Include legend only when comparing 3+ series; use direct labeling otherwise
labels_scales_colors.font_size: Minimum 12px for body text, 16px for KPI values, 20px for dashboard title
labels_scales_colors.accessibility: Ensure 4.5:1 contrast ratio; do not rely on color alone to convey meaning

interactions.filter_defaults: Default view: last 30 days, all segments

design_rationales.audience_adaptation: For beginner users: use simple KPI cards and avoid complex charts.
design_rationales.update_frequency_impact: With weekly updates, include a 'last updated' timestamp prominently.
design_rationales.chart_selection_principle: Charts were selected based on the primary user task: trend analysis → line charts; comparison → bar charts; part-to-whole → pie/donut; single value → KPI card.
design_rationales.cognitive_load: Limit to 5-7 visual elements per screen to avoid cognitive overload. Group related KPIs visually using whitespace and section headers.
design_rationales.data_ink_ratio: Remove chart borders, gridlines, and decorative elements. Every pixel should encode data or aid comprehension.

brief.title: Manufacturing Performance Dashboard
brief.business_goals: Analyze downtime causes and maintenance schedules
brief.data_context: Data sourced from internal manufacturing systems. Updated monthly. Covers the last 12 months with historical comparison.

recommendation.context_summary: This dashboard serves Customer Success Managers in the Manufacturing sector. The primary goal is to analyze downtime causes and maintenance schedules. Data is refreshed monthly, and the target users have advanced / expert data literacy. The design should prioritize clarity and actionability over analytical depth.

kpi_task_chart_mapping.rationale: A heatmap reveals patterns in On-Time Production Rate across two dimensions simultaneously.
Donut Chart is appropriate for the task of 'Compare across categories' with Energy Consumption per Unit.
A KPI card gives immediate visibility into the current Maintenance Cost value against its target.
A scatter plot exposes correlations between Safety Incidents and other variables.

layout_hierarchy.pattern: Single-page scrollable: Summary section → Trend section → Breakdown section → Details.
layout_hierarchy.primary_section: KPI scorecards for On-Time Production Rate, Energy Consumption per Unit
layout_hierarchy.secondary_section: Trend analysis for On-Time Production Rate over time

labels_scales_colors.color_palette: Healthcare White (#FFFFFF) with calming blue (#4A90D9) and green (#27AE60) for good metrics
labels_scales_colors.axis_labels: Always label axes with units (e.g., Revenue in EUR, Time in Days)
labels_scales_colors.number_format: Percentages: 1 decimal place (e.g., 23.4%). Large numbers: K/M suffix (e.g., 1.2M).
labels_scales_colors.scale_type: Linear for most KPIs; logarithmic if data spans multiple orders of magnitude
labels_scales_colors.legend: Include legend only when comparing 3+ series; use direct labeling otherwise
labels_scales_colors.font_size: Minimum 12px for body text, 16px for KPI values, 20px for dashboard title
labels_scales_colors.accessibility: Ensure 4.5:1 contrast ratio; do not rely on color alone to convey meaning

interactions.filter_defaults: Default view: last 30 days, all segments

design_rationales.data_ink_ratio: Remove chart borders, gridlines, and decorative elements. Every pixel should encode data or aid comprehension.
